$comment: Scope contract for in-session tarot. Every tarot-bearing rule found in the vault is enumerated once and classified: supported-v1 reaches the runtime catalog; deferred-preparation and not-applicable-non-tarot are audit-only and carry a rationale. Generated by md-procedures.mjs — see docs/rules/tarot-procedure-audit.md.

source.heading: Wood elf arête talent: Area Sense

entries.title: Job Board (Example Contracts)
entries.rationale: A preparation generator: "Draw ~5 cards to create a job board." The GM builds the board before the guild engages with it, so it is content creation rather than in-session resolution. Specification §9 defers it by name.

entries.title: City Creation — Districts
entries.rationale: GM preparation tooling. The boundary is the moment of use: generating a City before play is deferred, while the three Special City Actions that occur during play are supported.

entries.title: Underworld Creation
entries.rationale: GM preparation tooling. Distinct from the Augury spell (test-augury), which is cast during play; this is the Underworld-generation procedure of the same name.

entries.title: Flat 50% Chances
entries.rationale: Several Malediction curses and similar rules specify a flat "50% chance" without prescribing tarot. These stay GM-adjudicated and are never simulated with a card draw; the catalog models them as a manual-choice step inside oracle-malediction.

lookupTables.title: Hangover
lookupTables.$comment: Ch9:275 declares the convention for this table: every bracket refers to the top card of the minor arcana discard pile.
